channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Trump still protected from tax enforcement, but anti-weaponization fund is dead, Blanche says
item.description: The DOJ's $1.8 billion fund faced strong criticism because it could have compensated people convicted of crimes related to the Jan. 6, 2021, U.S. Capitol Riot.

item.title: Supreme Court allows Alabama to use congressional map that dilutes Black vote
item.description: The ruling is a victory for Republicans, who have sought to retain their slim majority in the House of Representatives by redistricting in various states.

item.title: Stock futures are little changed after S&P 500 closes at another record: Live updates
item.description: The three major indexes rose to fresh record highs on Tuesday.

item.title: Iran has mined 'large segments' of Hormuz Strait, Secretary of State Rubio says
item.description: Rubio called Iran's actions illegal in testimony to the Senate Foreign Relations Committee.

item.title: This income-generating asset offers yield exceeding 6% at a tax-advantaged rate
item.description: Investors who can stomach some rate volatility may be poised to pick up solid yield.

item.title: Oil climbs as U.S.-Iran trade strikes while Trump says negotiations with Tehran underway
item.description: U.S. Central Command said Tuesday that it had defeated multiple Iranian ballistic missiles following "attempted attacks" by Iran.

item.title: Japan's Nikkei hits record high as Asia markets rise amid Middle East concerns
item.description: Asia-Pacific markets opened higher even amid concerns over volatile oil prices with the Strait of Hormuz remaining closed.

item.title: Fed Chair Warsh makes first hires at central bank, including ‘Project 2025’ author
item.description: The new Fed chair has made his first hires. One wrote the Fed chapter in conservative policy blueprint "Project 2025."

item.title: China is making it harder for Mom and Pop to access U.S. stocks. Here's who will benefit
item.description: Chinese regulators recently tightened scrutiny on offshore brokerages including Futu and Tiger Brokers.

item.title: Dow jumps more than 200 points, S&P 500 posts first close above 7,600: Live updates
item.description: The S&P 500 ticked up to a record close after reaching a new all-time high on Tuesday.

item.title: Job openings in April surged to 7.6 million, the highest in nearly two years
item.description: The Bureau of Labor Statistics reported that available employment hit 7.6 million for the month, a surge of 731,000 from the prior month.

item.title: The top 10 highest-paying jobs for teens in the U.S.—No. 1 isn't retail or food service
item.description: Babysitting may not be your ultimate career path, but having a job as a teenager can build transferable skills for young workers, according to experts.

item.title: Jim Cramer says look to buy these 5 stocks outside the AI trade for diversification
item.description: CNBC's Jim Cramer said investors should consider adding exposure to out-of-favor sectors if investors begin rotating away from high-flying technology stocks.

item.title: Investor Joe Terranova buys these AI plays with 'strong momentum, strong fundamentals’
item.description: Investor Brian Belski also shared some recent stock picks on CNBC's 'Halftime Report' on Tuesday afternoon.

item.title: The CFTC has sparked a potential revolution on Wall Street. Exchange stocks are dropping
item.description: Investors are worried about increased competition for the traditional exchanges that have long dominated on Wall Street.

item.title: Palo Alto Networks tops earnings as AI fuels cybersecurity urgency
item.description: The beat comes on lowered expectations, after the company gave disappointing guidance in February that fell short of analyst estimates.

item.title: Greg Abel channels Buffett's deal-making style in nearly $17 billion spree, expanding into tech
item.description: The back-to-back deals offer one of the clearest signals yet that Abel is prepared to put Berkshire's enormous balance sheet to work.

item.title: California votes in primary to pick new governor, and maybe tip balance in Congress
item.description: California votes on Tuesday in a primary election that will choose two finalists for governor and Los Angeles mayor while also testing newly redrawn congressional lines that could tip the balance of power in the U.S. House of Representatives.

item.title: Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon says markets are in 'greed' mode as AI companies seek billions
item.description: Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon's comments come as investors prepare for what will be one of the busiest periods for equity issuance in years.

item.title: Microsoft unveils new AI models to lessen reliance on OpenAI and lower costs for developers
item.description: At its Build developer conference, Microsoft announced a series of generative AI models to try and crack a market controlled by OpenAI, Anthropic and Google.

item.title: We're upping our Palo Alto price target after strong earnings vanquish AI disruption fears
item.description: The business momentum is clear here, validating the stock's dramatic comeback to fresh highs.

item.title: Ending soon: Earn 150,000 miles with this all-time-high Capital One Venture Business offer
item.description: It's rare to see offers this large attached to credit cards with sub-$100 annual fees, like the Capital One Venture Business.

item.title: Ulta shares pop as beauty retailer beats Wall Street expectations and hikes earnings outlook
item.description: Ulta Beauty beat on the top and bottom lines for its fiscal first quarter on Tuesday as CEO Kecia Steelman praised the company's strategy.

item.title: Marvell stock soars 32% as Nvidia's Huang says it could be the next trillion-dollar company
item.description: Marvell Technology posted its best day ever after Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang made the "trillion-dollar" prediction.

item.title: Jeffries: Democrats aren't focused on Trump impeachment 'at this moment'
item.description: In conversation with CNBC's Emily Wilkins, the House leader laid out Democrats' path to a majority next Congress and plans to rein in President Donald Trump.

item.title: Trump names housing chief Bill Pulte acting intelligence director, replacing Tulsi Gabbard
item.description: Pulte has alleged wrongdoing related to mortgages against Trump's foes, Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook and New York Attorney General Letitia James.

item.title: Trump signs AI executive order asking companies to give government early access to models
item.description: AI developers, on a voluntary basis, are asked to collaborate with the government and provide early access to frontier models.

item.title: Traders say Karen Bass and Spencer Pratt will advance to runoff in high-profile L.A. mayoral race
item.description: Los Angeles voters go to the polls Tuesday. If no candidate receives more than 50%, the top-two vote getters will advance to a November runoff.

item.title: Why BlackRock's Rick Rieder feels 'a bit more relaxed' about AI bull market than dotcom era
item.description: 'I think you gotta stay in,' says the top BlackRock investing official, as earnings growth and cash to be reinvested in stocks support the AI bull market.

item.title: Audemars Piguet's watch prices remain stable after controversial Swatch collab
item.description: The Royal Pop collection of brightly colored watches on lanyards is part of AP's longer-term strategy of attracting the next generation of collectors.
